notes: Processed FBI-derived counts feed; the raw file duplicated North Carolina 2022 and omitted North Dakota 2022, so the mislabeled duplicate row was reassigned to North Dakota before panel construction.
Annual state resident population series from Census, used as the common population denominator for crime rates.
Monthly UR series annualized to annual average for balanced panels.
BEA per capita personal income series, kept nominal and deflated to 2024 dollars with CPI-U.
State-level demographic and socioeconomic controls; the long-run demographic panel uses ACS directly from 2008 onward and as the observed segment for educational attainment.
Age-race-sex-Hispanic origin state text files provide annual demographic shares before the 2000 intercensal CSVs.
Used to construct non-Hispanic race shares, male share, and age-group shares for 2000-2009.
Used to construct non-Hispanic race shares, male share, and age-group shares for 2010-2024.
Median household income and poverty rate; missing 1990-1992 and 1994 are interpolated within state to keep the panel balanced.
Educational attainment anchors used with ACS actuals to interpolate annual bachelor's-degree shares before 2008.
Annual state totals start in 1999 because 1998 is partial.
